---
title: "Registering a Sports Club in Flanders, Belgium"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    theme: readable
---

# Registering a Sports Club in Flanders, Belgium

To register a sports club in Flanders, Belgium, you need to establish a formal legal structure, register it with the Crossroads Bank for Enterprises, and affiliate with a local sports federation.

While you can technically operate a club as an informal group (*feitelijke vereniging*), setting up a non-profit organization, or vzw (*vereniging zonder winstoogmerk*), is highly recommended. A vzw protects your personal liability and is usually mandatory if you want to apply for local subsidies, secure sports infrastructure, or register for official leagues.

Follow these structured steps to fully register and set up your sports club.

## 1. Establish the Legal Framework: VZW Setup

Most formal sports clubs register as a vzw to ensure financial separation between the club and its founders.

### Draft the Statutes

You must write the club's bylaws (*statuten*). This must include the club name, purpose, address, and rules for general assembly meetings.

### Hold a Founding Meeting

Convene a general assembly with at least three founding members to approve the statutes and officially appoint the initial board of directors (*bestuursorgaan*).

### File the Incorporation Documents

Submit the approved statutes, minutes of the founding meeting, and board appointments to the local commercial court registry (*ondernemingsrechtbank*). You can complete this entirely online via the Belgian government's Just-on-web portal.

### Get a Company Number

Once approved, your vzw is automatically registered in the Crossroads Bank for Enterprises (*Kruispuntbank van Ondernemingen* or CBE) and assigned a unique company number. Your statutes will then be published in the *Belgisch Staatsblad* (Belgian Official Gazette).

## 2. Legal and Financial Obligations

Once your enterprise number is assigned, you must complete the following regulatory compliance steps.

### Register the UBOs

You are legally obligated to register all "Ultimate Beneficial Owners" — the board members who control the vzw — in the central Belgian UBO Register via MyMinfin within 30 days of incorporation.

### Open a Corporate Bank Account

You must establish a distinct, dedicated club bank account to cleanly handle membership fees, insurance premiums, equipment costs, and eventual subsidies.

## 3. Register with Local Authorities and Federations

To gain regional legitimacy, unlock funding, and schedule league matches, you must link your vzw to external sports networks.

### Affiliate with a Sports Federation

Find and join the recognized regional sports federation covering your specific discipline, such as Voetbal Vlaanderen for football or Tennis Vlaanderen. The Vlaamse Sportfederatie (VSF) provides complete step-by-step guides and resource toolkits for navigating this.

### Contact the Local Municipal Sports Department

Notify the local municipal sports office (*lokale sportdienst*) about your club. Doing so enables you to rent municipal sports halls at heavily discounted rates and qualifies you for municipal sports subsidies.

## 4. Set Up Insurance and Administration

### Arrange Sports Insurance

Your club must have a policy covering civil liability and physical accidents for all participants. Joining an official sports federation typically bundles a comprehensive, affordable insurance package into your annual membership.

### Leverage the Startup Bonus

As a newly forming sports club in Flanders, you can apply for up to three months of free sports insurance from Sport Vlaanderen to cover your pilot sessions and introductory workshops safely.

### Maintain the Member Registers

Keep a secure digital log of your active sporting members. For the internal governance of the vzw, you must also maintain an official written register indicating which members possess voting rights at your annual general assemblies.

# Yearly Obligations After Registration

Once your sports club is registered as a non-profit organization (vzw / asbl) in Belgium, you must complete several mandatory legal, administrative, and fiscal tasks every year to avoid heavy fines or a forced closure.

These yearly obligations are split into four main operational categories.

## 1. Administrative and Governance Requirements

Every year, you must convene your corporate entities and update internal records.

### Hold the General Assembly (AV)

You must organize at least one General Assembly of voting members within 6 months after your financial year ends. At this meeting, members must vote to approve the past year's accounts, vote on next year's budget, and discharge directors from liability.

### Hold a Board Meeting (BO)

The board of directors (*bestuursorgaan*) must meet at least once a year to draft the financial accounts and the upcoming budget before presenting them to the General Assembly.

### Maintain the Member Register

You must update your electronic or written list of voting members at the club's registered headquarters.

### Re-confirm the UBO Register

You are legally required to log into MyMinfin and confirm or update your "Ultimate Beneficial Owners" — the current board members — at least once a year, even if your board has not changed.

## 2. Financial and Accounting Duties

For most standard sports clubs classified as "micro" or "small" vzw's, the financial filings are relatively straightforward.

### Draw Up the Annual Accounts

You must compile a balance sheet showing your income, expenditures, assets, and liabilities.

### File the Approved Accounts

Within 30 days of your General Assembly approving the accounts, you must formally file them. "Micro" and "small" vzw's file these documents locally with the registry of the Commercial Court (*Ondernemingsrechtbank*), while larger clubs must file directly with the National Bank of Belgium (NBB).

## 3. Annual Tax Obligations

Even though sports clubs do not operate to make a profit, you are still required to handle annual corporate taxes.

### File the Legal Entities Tax (RPB)

You must submit a legal entity tax declaration via the Belgian government's digital Biztax platform. This must be submitted by the 7th month following the end of your financial balance sheet date.

### File the Patrimony Tax

If your sports club accumulates total assets — buildings, bank savings, equipment — valued at more than EUR 50,000, you are subject to an annual inheritance/patrimony tax (*patrimoniumtaks*). This declaration must be submitted by March 31 each year.

## 4. Federation and Local Compliance

### Renew Federation Affiliations

You must submit your active member roster to your regional sports federation, such as Voetbal Vlaanderen or Gymfed, by their specific seasonal deadline to renew individual athletic insurance policies.

### Submit Municipal Subsidy Reports

To keep receiving financial grants from your local municipal sports department (*lokale sportdienst*), you must submit an annual activity report and a copy of your approved accounts to the town hall.

# Deadline Planning Question

What month does your sports club use to close its financial year?

If you know this, you can map out the exact calendar deadlines for your upcoming General Assembly and tax filings.
